name: GoatCounter API
what_it_does: Minimalist open-source web analytics with REST API, offering privacy-friendly pageview tracking without cookies, GDPR overhead, or JavaScript requirements.
use_cases: Querying pageview counts and referrer data for lightweight reporting agents
Tracking bot and human visit ratios for content optimization
Monitoring traffic spikes for anomaly detection in personal projects
Fetching top page statistics for automated newsletter or report generation
Self-hosting analytics for maximum data ownership and privacy control
not_for: Enterprise analytics requiring user identification or session recording
E-commerce conversion tracking with funnel analysis
A/B testing or event-driven behavioral analytics
best_when: An agent needs basic traffic data for personal or indie projects where privacy-by-design and simplicity matter more than advanced features.
avoid_when: You need cohort analysis, event tracking, or any user-level behavioral data.

auth.notes: Single API token passed as 'Bearer' in Authorization header. Self-hosted instances use token from the instance settings. No scope system.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted: completely free. GoatCounter.com hosted: free for personal use
pricing.notes: Open-source (EUPL-1.2 with SaaS exception). Self-host for free on any VPS. Hosted version for personal use is free — one of the most generous free tiers in analytics.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: none. GoatCounter.com: reasonable limits not publicly documented

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is clear and concise for the limited endpoint set. Error responses are plain text or JSON with minimal detail. Very simple API surface.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only API for stats and pageview data. No write operations beyond tracking pixel hits.
known_agent_gotchas: API is primarily read-only; writing data requires direct database access on self-hosted
Data granularity is limited — no user-level or session-level data by design
Self-hosted instance API endpoint varies by deployment
Minimal library ecosystem — most use raw HTTP requests

security_score_components.security_notes: Open-source Go codebase is auditable. Privacy-by-design means no PII stored. Self-hosted instances control their own TLS configuration. Simple token model acceptable for low-sensitivity data.
